text: You are at a mountain cabin. Your host proudly announces there is no internet, no electricity, and no running water.
It is Taco Friday. The host realizes they forgot the Old El Paso spice mix. Panic ensues.
The municipality forgot to sand the icy sidewalks. It is a literal skating rink.
It is 13:30 on a Friday. Your manager casually packs their bag and says 'God helg!'
You are on a hiking trail. A stranger approaches. You must switch from 'City Mode' (ignore) to 'Mountain Mode' (say Hei).
You check the public tax records (Skattelister) and discover your lazy coworker makes 200,000 NOK more than you.
You drive two hours out of your way to avoid a 25 NOK toll booth.
It is May 17th. You are wearing a heavy wool Bunad. It is unexpectedly 24 degrees and sunny.
You are stopped at the Swedish border. Customs asks if you have anything to declare. You have 15 kilos of frozen bacon.
You run out of Vitamin D in November. The darkness begins to physically crush your soul.
A man in tight Lycra aggressively double-poles past you on the ski track, screaming 'Løype!'
You try to pay for a coffee with physical cash. The barista looks at the coins like they are ancient Roman artifacts.
A friend asks you to help them move. In Norway, this means carrying a grand piano down five flights of stairs for a slice of pizza.
You see a dog waiting outside a grocery store in the freezing snow. It looks happier than you do.
You open a bag of Sørlandschips on the quiet carriage of the train. The crunch echoes like a gunshot.
Your housing association announces a 'mandatory' Dugnad. You must scrub moss off the communal garbage bins.
You attempt to speak Norwegian to a local. They instantly reply in flawless, unaccented English.
You buy an overpriced hot dog at a gas station. They ask if you want 'rekesalat' (shrimp salad) on it.
A coworker takes an 'Egenmelding' (sick day) because their cat looked sad this morning.
It is exactly 0 degrees and raining. The snow turns into an inescapable, knee-deep brown slush (Slaps).
You see your stoic, humorless boss at Vinmonopolet buying 12 boxes of cheap boxed wine. It is 14:00 on a Tuesday.
The grocery store has a 'Tilbud' (sale). A block of Norvegia cheese is discounted by exactly 1 NOK. People are physically fighting over it.
It is mid-July. The local forecast issues a yellow warning for 'Fare for snø' (Danger of snow). You just bought a 3000 NOK hammock.
leftChoiceLabel: Set it up in the living room
rightChoiceLabel: Sleep in it during the blizzard

text: The office coffee machine breaks down. Within 45 minutes, society collapses and a primitive tribal system forms in the break room.
A ticket controller on the T-Bane stares at your digital ticket for an uncomfortably long time. You start doubting your own existence.
leftChoiceLabel: Confess to crimes you didn't commit

text: Your neighbor aggressively trims their hedge exactly 1 millimeter over your property line. It is a silent declaration of war.
You are invited to play padel tennis. You realize it's just an excuse for middle management to wear expensive shorts and drink IPA.
You buy a single plastic bag at Kiwi. The cashier asks if you need the receipt for your 4.50 NOK investment.
leftChoiceLabel: Demand the receipt for your taxes

text: You hold the door. They say 'Takk'. You say 'Vær så god'. They say 'Jo, takk'. You are trapped in an infinite politeness loop.
You send a sick note saying you have a minor cold. Your boss replies with a link to a 15km ski trail and says 'Get well soon'.
You attempt to make traditional Fårikål. Your apartment smells like wet wool and boiled depression for three consecutive weeks.
You slip on the ice, violently twisting your ankle. A passing Norwegian just yells 'Det er glatt ja!' (It's slippery, yes!) and keeps walking.
A Swedish tourist asks for directions in Swedish. You actually understand them, but national pride is at stake.
The bus is completely empty except for one person. You sit directly next to them to assert dominance.
You are buying a house. The real estate agent highlights the 'charming original 1970s linoleum floor'. Bidding starts at 6 million.
You are stuck in a broken elevator with a neighbor. The silence is so loud it causes actual physical pain in your eardrums.
The union threatens a strike because the office toilet paper was downgraded from 3-ply to 2-ply.
You mention you prefer downhill skiing over cross-country. A local accuses you of being a lazy, continental aristocrat.
You eat a Grandiosa frozen pizza with a knife and fork. A teenager films you and uploads it to TikTok. You are a national disgrace.
It is the darkest day of Mørketid. You stare into the void. The void stares back and asks if you have paid your NRK TV license.
You attend a baby shower. The parents have already bought the unborn infant a pair of 4000 NOK miniature cross-country skis.
An acquaintance tries to start a conversation. You jump onto a rental electric scooter and drive straight into the fjord to escape.
You find a 'First Price' brand parachute. It costs 50 NOK and is made of recycled plastic grocery bags.
You get a Vipps request for 0.20 NOK. The note says it is to cover the depreciation of the couch you sat on during the pre-party.
Your boss uses the word 'Synergi' four times in one sentence. You consider throwing yourself through the double-glazed window.
You try the seasonal 'Julemarsipan' (Christmas marzipan) that appeared in stores in September. You instantly age 15 years.
The wind in Bergen blows the rain straight up your nose. You are drowning on dry land.
You see a man wearing a perfectly tailored suit and a massive hiking backpack. He is jogging to work at an agonizing pace.
The train stops mid-journey. The conductor announces they have hit an elk, and the meat will be distributed to passengers.
You make 0.5 seconds of accidental eye contact with a stranger. According to local law, you must now move to a new municipality.
You try to claim a 500 NOK tax deduction for your home office. Skatteetaten (Tax Authority) audits your entire bloodline.
You buy a single artisan microbrew in Grünerløkka. It tastes like sour grapefruit and costs your firstborn child.
rightChoiceLabel: Pour it in the nearest potted plant

text: A local offers you 'Gammelost'. It literally translates to 'Old Cheese' and smells like an abandoned hockey locker room.
You go hiking. A dog passes you, carrying its own saddlebag. It is clearly in vastly superior cardiovascular shape than you.
The forecast says 'Delvis skyet' (Partly cloudy). This roughly translates to a Category 4 hurricane with intermittent hail.
You watch a Norwegian cut a slice of brown cheese. It is perfectly even, exactly 1mm thick, and curls like a ribbon. It is a work of art.
rightChoiceLabel: Hack at it with a knife

text: A teenager on a Voi electric scooter blows past you at 45 km/h, aggressively vaping and listening to terrible Swedish rap.
Your friend invites you to 'Fredagskos' but reveals they are making a salad instead of tacos. You feel profoundly betrayed.
A colleague has been on 'Sykemelding' (paid sick leave) for 8 months because they are 'finding themselves in Thailand'.
You are forced to use the self-checkout. The machine loudly accuses you of stealing an onion. A red light flashes.
rightChoiceLabel: Wait for the silent judgment of staff

text: You attempt to bake a traditional Kransekake. It collapses into a sticky, almond-flavored, apocalyptic ruin.
You step into what looks like a solid patch of snow. It is actually a knee-deep puddle of gray slush. Your soul leaves your body.
rightChoiceLabel: Take off your shoes on the bus

text: A Norwegian tries to tell a joke. It is a deeply tragic, 15-minute story about a lonely potato that freezes to death.
You complain about a minor uphill walk. A local reminds you of the WWII resistance fighters who skied across the country while starving.
You forget to validate your bus ticket. A controller doesn't fine you, but stares at you with pure, unadulterated disappointment.
leftChoiceLabel: Beg them to fine you instead

text: You see someone you vaguely know at the grocery store. You hide behind the Pant machine for 12 minutes to avoid them.
A meeting is scheduled for 16:00 on a Friday. Everyone assumes the company is going bankrupt and it's a mass firing event.
You buy 'Lørdagsgodt' (Saturday candy) on a Wednesday. You feel like a chaotic, unstoppable rebel.
You eat a hot dog wrapped in a potato pancake (Lompe). It is the most aggressively beige, flavorless meal in human history.
It is late November. The sun sets at 14:30. You put on your wool pajamas and accept that you will not feel warmth until May.
You receive a letter from Skatteetaten. They have calculated that you smiled twice last year. You owe 4000 NOK in 'Happiness Tax'.
A coworker asks 'Hvordan går det?'. You make the mistake of actually telling them about your divorce. They slowly back out of the room.
You reach Vinmonopolet at 14:59 on a Saturday. The manager locks the glass door while maintaining unbroken, dead-eyed eye contact.
You slip on the ice and lie paralyzed on the sidewalk. Passersby politely step over your body to respect your personal privacy.
You accidentally buy a single, non-discounted bell pepper. Your bank immediately sends an SMS asking if your card was stolen.
Your electric car's heater breaks in January. You must choose between freezing to death or arriving at work.
A Norwegian actively tries to brag about their child's achievements. Janteloven instantly vaporizes them into a cloud of humble dust.
You suggest to your Norwegian partner that you skip Taco Friday and eat Thai food. They begin packing their bags.
You are hiking uphill. A local passes you pushing a stroller, drinking coffee, and breathing through their nose.
rightChoiceLabel: Sit on a rock and weep

text: The municipality introduces a new toll (Bompenger) for looking at the fjord. It is automatically deducted from your account.
You attend a Julebord. The HR manager, who has not spoken all year, is currently shotgunning Aquavit and insulting the CEO.
You are cornered at a party. The only topic of conversation is the exact interest rate on their mortgage. You consider faking a stroke.
A stranger sits next to you on a completely empty bus. You must choose between screaming or violently jumping out the window.
You try to buy Paracetamol. The cashier treats you like a cartel kingpin trying to buy weapons-grade plutonium.
You return to your parked car. It is buried under two meters of solid ice by the municipal plow. The driver waves at you.
You are invited to 'Kos' at a cabin. It translates to sitting in a freezing room, eating dry crackers, and pretending you are not miserable.
You eat a hotdog at Narvesen. It costs 85 NOK and tastes exactly like boiled regret and salty depression.
You try to throw away a pizza box. The recycling bin is full. You must keep the box in your living room for the rest of your life.
leftChoiceLabel: Throw it in the general waste

text: The sun comes out in February for 12 seconds. Everyone stops working and stares at the window like a cult worshipping a dying god.
You complain about the 25 percent VAT on everything. A local traps you and explains why paved roads in Finnmark are worth your poverty.
You are at a pedestrian crossing. A car stops to let you pass. You must perform the mandatory awkward shuffle-jog to show gratitude.
Your boss schedules a mandatory 'fun' team-building event. It involves climbing a vertical ice wall at 6 AM.
You go to IKEA on a Saturday. You get stuck behind a family arguing about the structural integrity of the 'KALLAX' shelving unit.
You are offered a slice of 'Gammelost' (old cheese). It looks and smells like it survived the Black Death.
A Norwegian tells you they are just going for a 'little walk'. You end up 20 kilometers deep in a forest with no supplies.
It is -25 degrees. You check Yr.no. It proudly states 'føles som -35' (feels like -35). The app is actively mocking you.
You take the train. The conductor apologizes for a 2-hour delay because there are 'wrong types of leaves' on the track.
You accidentally brush hands with a stranger at the supermarket. They look at you as if you just proposed marriage.
You are invited to a cabin without a toilet. You must squat in the woods while a moose silently judges you.
You want to buy a single beer at a bar. The bartender hands you the card machine. The screen says 210 NOK.
Your colleague takes 6 weeks off because they feel 'slightly uninspired'. The company pays for their yoga retreat.
You try to eat a waffle with jam and sour cream. You spill it on your pants. A flock of seagulls instantly targets you.
The snow melts, revealing a thick layer of dog poop that was frozen since November. The city smells like biological warfare.
You ride a rental electric scooter over tram tracks. Your teeth vibrate so violently that two fillings fall out.
You attend a Dugnad. The housing board president forces you to pick weeds with a pair of tweezers for 4 hours.
Someone says 'Takk for sist' (Thanks for last time). You have absolutely no memory of ever meeting this person in your life.
You buy a 'cheap' used car. You then discover the annual road tax, insurance, and toll fees cost more than a private helicopter.
You try to quit your gym membership. SATS requires a written letter, a blood sacrifice, and a 3-month notice period.
You order a pizza in a restaurant. It arrives covered in heavily processed Bernaise sauce. The chef looks extremely proud.
It is raining horizontally. You see a woman walking a tiny Chihuahua. The dog is currently airborne like a furry kite.
A Norwegian tries to explain the rules of ski jumping. You realize it is just organized falling with style.
You are given a slice of Marzipan cake. It is 90 percent solid almond paste and 10 percent sponge. Your jaw locks up.
You are waiting for the bus. Someone stands 1.9 meters away instead of the mandatory 2 meters. You feel deeply violated.
You go on a Harrytur to Sweden. The border guard catches you with 3 bottles of wine instead of the allowed 2.
You park your car. You use the EasyPark app, but select the wrong zone by 4 meters. A 900 NOK fine immediately materializes.
Your union negotiates a pay raise. It is 3 percent. Inflation is 7 percent. They throw a waffle party to celebrate the massive victory.
You try to squeeze Kaviar from a tube. The tube violently ruptures, spraying salty fish eggs all over your clean shirt.
The Yr.no app promises sun. You go for a walk. A sudden blizzard traps you in a forest. Yr.no updates to 'Blizzard' retroactively.
You mention that Swedish meatballs are better than Norwegian meatballs. A crowd forms. They are carrying torches.
You reach day 1170. You have not spoken to a stranger in 3 years. You eat brown cheese in the dark. You are perfectly happy.
You contact NAV for a simple question about your sick leave. The automated phone menu has 47 options and ends by hanging up on you.
You are standing at a bus stop. Another person arrives and stands 1.8 meters away. You feel intensely crowded and violated.
You are offered a tube of Baconost. It is a terrifying meat-cheese paste that looks like industrial sealant.
You board a Vy train. The conductor apologizes that the train is delayed due to a 'cow on the tracks'. Again.
rightChoiceLabel: Sigh and wait for the beef

text: The Yr app says it will be sunny. You leave your heavy jacket at home. You are immediately struck by horizontal hail.
Your neighbor installs a flagpole. You are now locked in a silent, bitter cold war over who has the straightest, greenest hedge.
You buy a single piece of sushi at the airport. It costs 180 NOK. You chew it for 45 minutes to maximize the financial value.
You join a 'casual' corporate 5k run. Your CEO shows up in carbon-plated shoes, aerodynamic sunglasses, and a heart rate monitor.
You make a joke in a crowded elevator. The other passengers stare forward, mentally calculating your threat level.
It is Friday. You are legally obligated to eat tacos. You attempt to make a burrito instead. Your friends gasp in horror.
rightChoiceLabel: Crush it into a taco shell

text: You buy spikes for your shoes (Brodder). You walk into the grocery store sounding like a heavily armored medieval knight.
You are served Raspeballer. They are dense, grey potato dumplings that sit in your stomach like undigested lead weights.
You ask your boss for a raise. They offer you a slightly larger coffee mug and a vague promise of 'synergy'.
You travel to Sweden on a Harrytur. You return with 40 cans of energy drink, 5 kilos of cheese, and a deep sense of shame.
An electric scooter is parked horizontally across your front door. You are physically trapped inside your own home.
You accidentally 'Reply All' to a company-wide email with a personal complaint. You must now change your name and move to Svalbard.
A Norwegian offers you a 'Klem' (hug). It is as rigid, brief, and emotionless as two wooden boards colliding in the wind.
You experience the Midnight Sun. It is 4 AM and you are vacuuming the living room with frantic, unstoppable energy.
You put ketchup on a traditional Christmas sausage. Your Norwegian in-laws physically remove your plate from the table.
You try ice skating on a frozen lake. You fall immediately. A passing swan seems to laugh at your lack of core strength.
A meeting is scheduled for 15:45 on a Friday. You assume it is a hostile corporate takeover or a mass firing event.
You are asked to bring a cake to a Dugnad. You bring a store-bought Toro mix. You are exiled from the housing board.
You try to buy a house in Oslo. Bidding starts at 7 million NOK for a 20-square-meter basement with a shared toilet.
You accidentally sit in someone's unassigned assigned seat at work. They stand awkwardly nearby, sighing heavily until you move.
It is -12 degrees. You see a group of kindergarteners sleeping outside in strollers. They are invincible snow mutants.
You eat a whole bag of Smash. You realize the salty-sweet corn cones are superior to all other candy. You are ready to defend this fact.
You drive through a toll ring (Bompenger) just to go to the grocery store. Your carton of milk now effectively costs 95 NOK.
You visit a cabin. The host proudly demonstrates the 'bio-toilet'. It is essentially a bucket of sawdust and despair.
Someone says 'Takk for maten' (Thanks for the food) after eating a single dry cracker. You reply 'Vær så god' with equal solemnity.
You catch yourself complaining about the Swedes. You don't even know why anymore. It is just a primal, geographical instinct.
Your office institutes a 'no-shoe' policy. You must walk around in your socks. One of your socks has a massive, embarrassing hole in the toe.
The snow finally melts, revealing the metric tons of gravel spread on the ice all winter. The city is now a giant, dusty sandbox.
You buy a bottle of water. You keep the empty bottle in your backpack for 6 months because you refuse to lose the 3 NOK Pant.
You are offered Lutefisk. You swallow it whole without chewing so the toxic lye jelly doesn't dissolve your tongue.
You join a local SATS gym. You realize everyone there looks like a sculpted Norse deity. You lift a 2kg pink dumbbell and go home.
It is Easter. You are forced to eat an unpeeled orange and a Kvikk Lunsj at the top of a freezing, wind-swept mountain.
You try to split a restaurant bill. Your friend pulls out a calculator to ensure you pay for exactly your 3.4 slices of pizza and half a cola.
You take the night bus home. It is a rolling biohazard of crushed kebab wrappers, spilled garlic sauce, and questionable life choices.
Your boss tells you to 'ta det med ro' (take it easy). You spend the rest of the week in a paranoid spiral, questioning if you are being fired.
The first snow of the year falls. Traffic instantly collapses across the entire country, as if snow is a brand new, alien phenomenon.
You eat a slice of Jarlsberg. It is the most aggressively mild, unoffensive cheese in existence. It literally tastes like the color white.
You try to return a sweater. The cashier asks for your BankID, your Vipps history, your birth certificate, and your blood type.
You are invited to a confirmant's party. The 14-year-old gives a detailed PowerPoint presentation on how they will invest their 70,000 NOK gift money.
You wave to a bus driver as you cross the street. They do not wave back. They do not blink. You consider moving to a different city.
You are walking on the sidewalk. A Tesla silently creeps up directly behind you like a stealth predator and nearly gives you a heart attack.
The wind chill is -20°C. You wear 3 layers of wool. You step into a shopping mall heated to 28°C. You begin to physically melt.
You take an 'Egenmelding' (sick day) because you have a slight headache. The welfare state fully supports your brave decision.
You drink a glass of Solo. It tastes like liquid yellow nostalgia and aggressively orange-flavored sugar.
You watch 'Grevinnen og Hovmesteren' (Dinner for One) on Little Christmas Eve. You do not laugh, but you watch the entire thing out of sheer duty.
You survive 1220 days. You finally understand that silence on a bus is not awkward; it is a warm, comforting, anti-social blanket.
You apply for sick leave because your soul has left your body. NAV (Welfare) rejects your application, demanding a doctor's note proving you had a soul to begin with.
A stranger sits next to you on a completely empty bus. Logically, you must conclude they are an assassin sent to end your life.
The wind in Bergen is so strong it threatens to blow your depression away. Instead, it blows a wet seagull directly into your face.
You buy a 75 NOK black coffee. The barista flips the screen to ask for a 20 percent tip. You consider offering your internal organs.
Your neighbor is vacuuming their lawn during the spring Dugnad. You realize you live next to a highly functioning psychopath.
The 'Buss for tog' (train replacement bus) driver takes a wrong turn and seems to be driving directly into a fjord. Nobody says a word.
You buy an organic cucumber wrapped in three layers of thick plastic. You throw away the cucumber and eat the plastic. It has more flavor.
At the SATS gym, a muscular man drops a 100kg barbell directly on his foot. His foot shatters. He politely whispers 'Uff da'.
You make unbroken eye contact with a Norwegian on the street. They immediately call the police to report a psychic assault.
The HR department introduces 'Mandatory Fun Time'. You are legally obligated to smile for 15 minutes while eating a dry waffle.
You check Yr.no. It predicts 'Oppholdsvær' (dry). You step outside and a frozen cod falls from the sky, knocking you unconscious.
You are offered a plate of Smalahove. The dead sheep's eye stares deep into your soul, violently judging your life choices.
rightChoiceLabel: Eat the eye to assert dominance

text: You use a Swedish word by accident during a meeting. You are immediately stripped of your Norwegian residence permit and exiled.
The Pant machine violently rejects your bottle. You scream at the machine. It prints a receipt that just says 'Seek Therapy'.
You try to board the tram before the passengers exit. A 90-year-old woman physically tackles you to the pavement.
rightChoiceLabel: Lie in the gutter in shame

text: You slip on black ice and fall into a coma. You wake up in the hospital in late May. It is still snowing.
You put ketchup on a traditional Christmas Ribbe (pork belly). Your Norwegian father-in-law instantly writes you out of his will.
You receive a Vipps request for 0.01 NOK. The note says it is compensation because you inhaled some of your friend's indoor oxygen.
A colleague leaves work at 14:00 to 'pick up their kids in kindergarten'. You know they are single and childless. You respect the lie.
You survive 1240 days in Norway. You suddenly realize you have not felt a single human emotion since late 2022.
You buy 8000 NOK expedition-grade hiking boots. You only use them to walk 400 meters to Kiwi to buy frozen pizza. You feel like Amundsen.
The sun finally comes out. You take off your shirt in the park. You somehow get a severe sunburn and frostbite simultaneously.
You try to buy wine at 15:01 on a Saturday. The Vinmonopolet doors are locked. You press your face against the glass and lick it in despair.
Someone holds the door open for you from 50 meters away. You must now sprint the entire distance to avoid being perceived as rude.
rightChoiceLabel: Walk at a painfully slow pace

text: You send an urgent work email. The auto-reply reads: 'I am on sick leave due to mild existential dread. Back in November.'
You park your car in downtown Oslo. The parking fee is automatically deducted from your unborn grandchildren's future inheritance.
You eat a Grandiosa pizza and discover a single piece of paprika that actually contains flavor. It is a culinary miracle.
You go to a cabin. There is no Wi-Fi, no water, and no heat. You stare at a wooden log for 48 hours. They call this 'Kos'.
It is raining so aggressively in Bergen that the water is falling upwards. You begin to drown while standing on your third-floor balcony.
You tell a coworker they look nice today. They recoil in absolute horror, assuming you are either mocking them or trying to scam them.
You go on a Harrytur to Sweden. You buy so much cheap bacon and Pepsi Max that your car's rear suspension literally snaps in half.
You pass a toddler on the ski slopes. The toddler challenges you to a race, beats you effortlessly, and calls you weak in fluent Nynorsk.
The office union strikes because the free coffee beans were downgraded from 'Premium Dark' to 'Standard Gold'. The entire country shuts down.
A cyclist in full Lycra runs a red light and hits you on a crosswalk. They aggressively demand you apologize for denting their 80,000 NOK bike.
rightChoiceLabel: Push their bike into the fjord

text: You try to eat a waffle loaded with three thick slices of brown cheese. The cheese instantly cements your jaws together permanently.
The wind chill is -30. You see a teenager waiting for the bus wearing shorts, ankle socks, and an open jacket. Natural selection is failing.
You attempt the 'Utvask' (moving out clean). The landlord withholds your 15,000 NOK deposit because they found a single dust mite behind the radiator.
You say 'Hei' to a stranger deep in the forest. They say 'Hei' back. According to rural Norwegian law, you are now legally married.
You buy a hot dog at a Circle K gas station. You must take out a second mortgage on your apartment to afford the crispy onions.
It is mid-July. You are the only person left in the office building. You put your feet on the desk and declare yourself the new CEO.
You are served Lutefisk. You slowly realize it is just fish-flavored Jell-O made with industrial drain cleaner. They are watching you eat it.
You buy a daylight lamp to survive Mørketid. You stare directly into it until your retinas burn away. You still feel incredibly sad.
You attend a Dugnad. The board tasks you with painting a fence that was literally painted last Tuesday. You do not question it.
You try to cross the street at a zebra crossing. A Tesla driver accelerates rapidly just to assert their electric dominance over pedestrians.
A friend invites you to 'Vorspiel'. You arrive and five people are sitting in a circle, drinking lukewarm Hansa in absolute, terrifying silence.
You forget a reusable bag at the store. To avoid paying the 4 NOK plastic bag fee, you carry 15 wet items in your jacket like a pregnant kangaroo.
You casually mention at a dinner party that cross-country skiing is a bit boring. You are immediately placed on a deportation watchlist.
You ask your boss for feedback. They say 'Det er greit' (It is fine). You spend the next month overanalyzing their tone at 3 AM.
The snow finally melts in April. The streets are now covered in 6 months of hidden, thawed dog poop. You must play a lethal game of hopscotch.
You have lived in Norway so long you start finding Swedes incredibly annoying for absolutely no logical reason. The transformation is complete.
A Tesla driver aggressively tailgates your 2004 Toyota. You brake check them. They flash their high beams and write a passive-aggressive Facebook post about you.
You attempt to compliment a colleague's work. They immediately deny any skill, attributing their success entirely to the team, the coffee machine, and the weather.
The Yr app predicts 'Lett bris' (light breeze). A neighbor's trampoline flies past your 4th-floor window at 90 km/h.
You try to buy a single piece of fresh fruit at a convenience store. It costs roughly the same as a domestic flight to Bergen.
You are walking in the forest. You see an acquaintance approaching. You must quickly decide whether to veer off into a swamp to avoid them.
You try to use physical cash at a coffee shop. The barista looks at you with a mix of profound pity and absolute terror.
You watch a 60-year-old Norwegian man swim in the fjord in January. He isn't shivering. He looks incredibly bored.
A meeting is scheduled to end at 11:30. At exactly 11:28, the participants begin loudly zipping their bags to assert their right to leave.
You step outside in November. The darkness is so thick it feels like physical pressure on your chest. You wonder if the sun ever existed.
leftChoiceLabel: Chew on a Vitamin D pill

text: You are at a cabin. The only food left is a tube of bacon cheese and a pack of dry crispbread. This must sustain you for 48 hours.
You hold the door open for a stranger. They slip through without saying 'Takk'. You spend the next decade wishing them a slow, painful downfall.
You are on the T-Bane. Someone's phone rings loudly. The entire carriage collectively inhales in horror at this massive breach of the social contract.
You buy a Bunad to fit in. It costs 40,000 NOK. You wear it on May 17th, immediately spill ketchup on it, and ruin your life financially.
leftChoiceLabel: Try to wash it in the sink

text: You go for a 'short' run. A local tells you the path is 'bortover' (flat). You quickly realize it is a 45-degree incline up a jagged cliff.
You ask for a promotion. Your boss explains that promoting you would upset the flat hierarchy and make the underperformers feel bad.
The Pant machine accepts your bottle, but the printer is out of paper. You have lost 3 NOK forever. Your financial portfolio will never recover.
The ice is melting, revealing a horrifying amount of gravel. The 'spring cleaning' street sweepers spread a toxic dust cloud over the entire city.
A Norwegian friend invites you to dinner. They serve plain boiled potatoes, boiled carrots, and boiled white fish. There is no sauce. Only water.
You try to cross the street on a green light. A cyclist refuses to yield, yelling that they have the right of way while blowing through a red light.
You accidentally bump shoulders with someone. You both immediately apologize profusely, then walk away in opposite directions to die of embarrassment.
You send a work message on Slack at 16:02. You are immediately ostracized by the team for not respecting the sacred work-life balance.
You go to Vinmonopolet and ask the clerk for a recommendation. They trap you in a 20-minute, intensely detailed lecture on soil acidity in the Loire Valley.
The bus driver closes the doors exactly as you reach them. He makes prolonged eye contact, shakes his head slowly, and drives away into the darkness.
You discover that 'Fredagskos' is not a mere suggestion; it is a rigid, mandatory societal structure holding the entire country together.
It is pouring rain. You open a cheap umbrella. A Norwegian walks past in a 12,000 NOK Arcteryx jacket and audibly laughs at your flimsy shield.
You eat a Grandiosa pizza. You accidentally bite into the single, solitary piece of green paprika on the crust. It tastes like frozen despair.
You are cornered at a party by someone painstakingly explaining the intricate differences in the wood paneling between their two cabins.
You try to ski down a gentle slope. You immediately perform an involuntary split, tearing your groin muscles. A toddler points and laughs.
You buy a single plastic bag at the supermarket. The government immediately places a lien on your property.
You survive 1300 days in Norway. You no longer feel the cold. You no longer desire human interaction. You are pure, concentrated Kos.
You receive a tax bill that demands your firstborn child. You calculate the math and realize it is still cheaper than buying a pint of beer at Aker Brygge.
A teenager wearing an unzipped Canada Goose jacket complains to you about the heat. It is 4 degrees Celsius and hailing.
You discover a forgotten slice of brown cheese in the back of the fridge. It has calcified into a weapon-grade solid.
You are invited to a highly exclusive 'Fredagskos'. It consists of eating dry chips and watching a slow-paced crime drama in utter, terrifying silence.
A cyclist in full Tour de France spandex runs a red light, hits your car, and demands you pay for his 80-NOK organic espresso he dropped.
rightChoiceLabel: Throw his bike in the fjord

text: Your neighbor builds a neat snow fort. By unwritten Norwegian law, you must now build a slightly larger, yet aggressively humble, snow fort.
The coffee machine breaks. Within 45 minutes, the flat office hierarchy collapses into a violent, primitive tribal system.
You fall asleep on the T-Bane and wake up at the end of the line in a dark, terrifying pine forest. The conductor simply says 'God tur'.
You offer a colleague a piece of chewing gum. They immediately Vipps you 0.30 NOK to clear the suffocating financial debt.
The local supermarket announces a 'Priskrig' (Price War). A single avocado is now 38 NOK instead of 39 NOK. A riot breaks out.
You find a 100 NOK bill on the street. You must leave it there because picking it up would imply you think you deserve it more than others.
You complain about the freezing rain. A local reminds you that their ancestors survived the Ice Age here without complaining.
You try to make small talk in a crowded elevator. The person next to you pushes the emergency stop button to escape the interaction.
You are served a waffle with brown cheese. You ask for a fork. The host stares at you and slowly asks you to leave their house.
A massive moose blocks the entrance to your office building. You email your boss. Your boss replies: 'The moose is your manager now.'
You attend a 14-year-old's confirmation party. The teenager gives a 20-minute PowerPoint presentation analyzing their diverse index fund portfolio.
You buy a cheap umbrella. The Bergen wind instantly turns it into a lethal projectile that takes out a flock of seagulls.
You absentmindedly put your feet on the empty train seat across from you. A pensioner glares at you so hard your soul temporarily leaves your body.
The sun comes out in November. It lasts for exactly 14 seconds before hiding behind a thick, grey cloud of eternal sorrow.
You try to buy wine on a Sunday. The automated glass doors of Vinmonopolet remain locked, actively judging you for your poor life planning.
You go to a cabin with no electricity, water, or insulation. You spend the weekend staring at a single candle. You are forced to call it 'Koselig'.
You are invited to join the office 'Isbad' (ice bathing) club. You realize your coworkers are actively seeking the sweet release of hypothermia.
You buy a hot dog at a gas station. The cashier asks if you want crispy onions. You say no. They look at you like a serial killer.
You try to cross the street. A Tesla driver accelerates silently, attempting to assert the electric hierarchy of the road over your fragile bones.
You receive an email from NAV regarding your sick pay. It simply says 'Please wait'. You wait for three consecutive years.
You try to eat a Grandiosa pizza fresh out of the oven with your bare hands. The greasy cheese permanently burns off your fingerprints.
You go on a Harrytur to Sweden. You buy so much cheap candy and bacon that your car's suspension literally fails at the border crossing.
You try to speak Norwegian at a cafe. The barista immediately replies in perfect, aristocratic British English, destroying your confidence forever.
You accidentally step onto the red bicycle lane in Oslo. A cyclist rings their bell. It sounds like the tolling of the apocalypse.
You are invited to the housing board Dugnad. You are handed a pair of tweezers and told to remove moss from between the paving stones.
You eat a piece of Lutefisk. You realize you have just ingested industrial-grade cleaning chemicals disguised as fish. Your stomach begins to dissolve.
You try to leave a party. The 'doorway goodbye' involves putting on 4 layers of wool while talking about the weather for 45 minutes.
You complain about the 150 NOK Bompenger (toll). A local enthusiastically explains how your money is funding a 10km tunnel for a single sheep.
You see a child sleeping outside in a stroller in -15 degrees. The child looks vastly more well-rested and powerful than you ever will.
You accidentally hold the door for someone 20 meters away. They perform a panicked jog to reach you and tear their Achilles tendon.
You try to buy a single bell pepper. The cashier weighs it and tells you it costs more than a premium Netflix subscription.
You are asked to chop wood at the cabin. You swing the axe with immense confidence and immediately lodge it deep into your own shin.
leftChoiceLabel: Bleed quietly to not ruin Kos

text: You attend an office Julebord. Your strict, silent manager drinks a bottle of Aquavit and challenges a potted fern to a fistfight.

You are forced to eat a Matpakke (packed lunch) consisting of dry kneipp bread and sweaty white cheese. You weep silently in the break room.
You try to return a bottle for Pant. The machine rejects it with a loud, aggressive beep. You feel the collective shame of the entire supermarket.
leftChoiceLabel: Throw it in the regular trash

text: You complain about the dark winter in Oslo. A Tromsø native overhears you and claims they haven't seen the sun since 1998.

text: You complain about the price of a beer (110 NOK). A local defensively explains that high alcohol taxes are a public health measure to stop you from having fun.
