text: You have survived 2250 days in Norway. You are given a permanent right to complain about the price of a beer.
You inherit a 'charming' traditional Norwegian cabin deep in the mountains. Do you inspect it first, or just accept the keys blindly?
You arrive at your new cabin. The roof is leaking, there is no electricity, and a sheep is sleeping in the kitchen. Do you hire a Norwegian carpenter or fix it yourself?
You fixed the roof, but now the outhouse is frozen solid. It is -20 degrees outside.
You finally return to civilization. The toll booths on the way back charge you 800 NOK because you drove a diesel car.
You are invited to the housing cooperative's spring Dugnad (mandatory community labor). You hate raking leaves.
You are at the Dugnad, but you brought store-bought muffins instead of homemade waffles. The neighborhood committee board is glaring at you.
To redeem yourself with the neighbors, you are assigned the worst job: cleaning the communal gutters filled with mysterious black sludge.
Your boss demands you work overtime on a Friday. In Norway, the office empties at exactly 15:00. Do you stay or leave?
You are 'burnt out' from working 37.5 hours a week. You get a 'Sykmelding' (paid sick leave). What do you do with your time off?
You posted a picture of yourself skiing in the Alps while on sick leave. Your boss saw it. You are now dealing directly with NAV.
It is December. The legendary company Julebord (Christmas Party) approaches. The theme is 'Ugly Sweaters and Aquavit'.
You are severely intoxicated at the Julebord. The CEO is giving a boring speech about Q3 profits. You feel the urge to interrupt.
You wake up the next day in a snowbank holding a half-eaten frozen kebab. You have 30 missed calls from HR.
HR insists you attend a mandatory 'workplace boundaries' seminar, held in a windowless basement in Drammen.
It is May 16th. You realize your traditional Bunad is two sizes too small or you don't own one. The national pressure to look perfect is suffocating.
It's 7:00 AM on the 17th of May. You are at a 'Champagne Breakfast'. You've already had four glasses and need to give a speech to people you barely know.
You are in the middle of the parade crowd. Your bladder is screaming. Every public toilet has a 45-minute queue of crying children and angry parents.
The parade is over. You lost your wallet and the trains are canceled. Your only way home is a 'Buss for Tog' that may never arrive.
November hits. The 'Great Darkness' has arrived. You decide to find a partner on Tinder purely to have someone to share the soaring electricity bills with.
Your first date is a 10km hike in pitch darkness with headlamps. They ask if you enjoy putting 'Gudbrandsdalsost' on your waffles.
The date went well. You are meeting their parents in a remote village. They serve 'Smalahove' (boiled sheep's head). Your date's father is watching you.
You bought 12,000 NOK worth of carbon-fiber ski gear to look professional at Sognsvann. A 5-year-old child in a 20-year-old jacket just overtakes you uphill.
While trying to out-ski the 5-year-old, you lose control and crash into a frozen birch tree. You are lying in a snowbank and your pride is shattered.
You fly to Tromsø in January specifically to see the Northern Lights. It has been cloudy for 14 days and a small beer costs 165 NOK.
You pay for a 'Professional Aurora Chase' bus. The guide drives you to a dark parking lot behind a REEMA 1000 and tells you to 'wait and pray'.
You finally see a faint, blurry green smudge in the sky. Is it the Aurora Borealis or just light pollution from a nearby fish factory?
It is raining sideways. You drop your kid off at the Barnehage and realize you forgot their 'Regntøy' (rain gear). The head teacher looks at you like a criminal.
You bought the most expensive Helly Hansen gear to prove you're a good parent. Now you must label every single item with the kid's name or they will be lost forever in the 'Glemt-kassen'.
The gear is labeled and on the kid. 5 minutes later, they jump into a knee-deep mud puddle. The waterproof seal fails immediately.
Friday night. You decide to perform the sacred Norwegian ritual: Eating a 'Pizza Grandiosa'. Do you add extra cheese or eat it as the ancestors intended?
You are performing the second part of the ritual: meticulously picking off the tiny red bell pepper bits because nobody actually likes them.
You finished the pizza. You realize you left the oven on for 3 hours. Your smart-meter app shows the electricity price just spiked to 8 NOK per kWh.
It is Saturday, 14:45. The Vinmonopolet closes in 15 minutes. If you fail now, you will be sober at tonight’s party while everyone else discusses their mortgage.
You made it inside at 14:59. You grab a random bottle of red wine. At the register, the clerk asks for your ID. You are 38 years old, but forgot your wallet in the car.
The clerk refused you. You walk out empty-handed. A 'friendly' teenager outside offers to buy you a bottle for a 500 NOK 'convenience fee'.
Your hallway is blocked by three massive bags of empty beer cans. Your partner is threatening to leave you unless you 'Pant' them today.
You are at the recycling machine. It's working perfectly until the last can, which causes a 'System Error: Call Staff'. A line of five angry pensioners forms behind you.
The machine is fixed. You have a receipt for 142 NOK. Do you keep the money or press the 'Pantelotteriet' button to donate it for a 0.0001% chance at a million?
You enter the office kitchen and find a half-eaten 'Matpakke' left inside the microwave. The smell is… biological. Do you clean it?
You left a passive-aggressive Post-it note on the microwave: 'Whoever did this is a monster.' Ten minutes later, your boss is crying at their desk.
Your boss calls a mandatory 'Kitchen Culture' workshop. You must spend 4 hours discussing how to 'share the space' with a certified Norwegian consultant.
Your colleagues are all signing up for the 'Birkebeiner'—a 54km ski race over a mountain. To avoid looking weak, you sign up too. You haven't skied since 2014.
It's race day. The temperature is exactly 0 degrees—the waxer's nightmare. Your skis are sticking to the snow like industrial Velcro. Everyone is gliding past you.
You finish the race 9 hours late. The finish line is being dismantled. A frostbitten volunteer hands you a participation medal that looks like a bottle cap.
You are hunting for an apartment in Oslo. You find a 'studio' for 16,000 NOK. The bed is in the kitchen and you have to share a shower with a family of four.
Your landlord, a man named Thor who wears socks with sandals, informs you that 'guests' are prohibited and the internet is limited to checking the weather.
Thor catches you using a toaster after 21:00, which 'disturbs the communal peace'. He invokes a secret clause in your contract to raise the rent.
You board an empty bus. Distracted by your phone, you sit in the double seat right next to the only other passenger. You feel their soul leave their body in terror.
The passenger is so uncomfortable they get off the bus 4 kilometers before their actual stop just to escape the 'Bus Talker'.
A video of your 'Bus Incident' goes viral on a Norwegian subreddit. You are now known as 'The Personal Space Invader'.
Your boss approves a 'Hyttekontor' (working from the cabin). You drive 5 hours into the wilderness, promising to be 'fully available' on Slack.
A massive blizzard hits. The 4G tower freezes over exactly 2 minutes before your 'Strategic Alignment' presentation with the board of directors.
The internet is back, but you are now on a local news segment titled 'The Idiot on the Roof' after a neighbor filmed your signal-hunting attempt.
It is 'Smalahove' season. Your traditionalist in-laws serve you a scorched sheep's head. The eye is staring directly into your soul.
To wash down the sheep's head, your father-in-law pours you a triple shot of room-temperature Linje Akevitt. He won't blink until you finish it.
The combination of sheep fat and Akevitt has triggered a biological emergency. The only bathroom is right next to the dining table with paper-thin walls.
Electricity prices are at an all-time high. Your 'Smart Home' app tells you the cheapest time to use the washing machine is Tuesday at 03:15 AM.
You are doing laundry in the dark at 3 AM to save 4 NOK. You trip over a stray wool sock and fall into the cold, damp basement floor.
Your neighbor saw you lying on the basement floor through the window. They didn't help, but they did post about 'suspicious activity' in the neighborhood Facebook group.
You are at an office lunch. You use a regular knife to slice the communal block of 'Brunost' (Brown Cheese) instead of the sacred Ostehøvel (cheese slicer). The room gasps in horror.
HR calls you in for a 'cultural realignment' meeting. They place three different cheese slicers on the desk and demand you identify the specific 'Fløtemysost' slicer.
You failed the cheese slicer test. As penance, your boss sends you to a remote mountain farm to hand-carve a wooden Ostehøvel from a birch tree.
It is a long weekend. You embark on a 'Harrytur' (cross-border shopping trip to Sweden) to buy cheap bacon, snus, and Pepsi Max.
Your trunk is packed with 80 liters of cheap Swedish energy drinks. You approach the Norwegian border toll. The customs officer (Toller) makes direct eye contact.
You are pulled over. You are over the legal import quota by 12 liters of soda and 4 kg of meat. The Toller starts writing a massive ticket.
You live in a block of flats. You are exactly 3 minutes late to retrieve your clothes from the communal 'Vaskekjeller'. A neighbor has already placed your wet underwear on top of the machine.
You left a passive-aggressive Post-it note. The next day, there is a fully laminated, two-page reply citing 'Paragraph 4, Section B' of the building's laundry bylaws.
At the 'Sameiemøte' (tenant meeting), nobody brings up the laundry war. Instead, the entire building spends 3 hours debating if the gravel in the driveway should be 'grey' or 'dark grey'.
It is only October 15th. The local Kiwi supermarket just put out the first batch of 'Julebrus' (Christmas Soda). A grown man is frantically hoarding 40 crates.
You secured a single glass bottle of Dahls Julebrus. You step outside and immediately drop it on the icy asphalt. It shatters. A crowd gathers, taking off their beanies in mourning.
A local newspaper writes an article about your 'Julebrus Tragedy'. You are forced to go on national TV and debate the most dangerous topic in Norway: Is Brown or Red Julebrus superior?
Tired of paying massive toll ring fees, you buy a brand new Tesla. It costs your entire life savings, but you feel incredibly morally superior.
You are driving your EV to a remote cabin. It is -25°C. The battery suddenly drops from 40% to 2% in three minutes. You are in the middle of nowhere.
Your EV dies. You are rescued by a local farmer driving a massive, polluting 1998 diesel Volvo. He tows you back to civilization while laughing loudly at you.
You try to call NAV (the welfare administration) to ask a simple question about your tax bracket. The automated voice says you are number 412 in the queue.
You finally get through. The representative sighs heavily and informs you that you need 'Form 24-B', which must be submitted via fax or pigeon.
You found a fax machine in a museum and sent the form. A week later, you receive a letter stating you filled it out in blue ink instead of black. Claim denied.
You bring a hot lunch to the office instead of the traditional 'Matpakke' (dry bread with cheese). The smell of warm food deeply offends your colleagues.
HR issues a passive-aggressive memo about 'odorous food disruptions.' Everyone is staring at your microwaved fish.
To conform, you switch to eating dry Kneippbrød with a single, sweating slice of Salami. Your soul is dying, but your social standing is fully restored.
Your athletic, overly-cheerful colleague convinces you to join them for an 'Isbading' (ice bathing) session in the Oslo fjord.
You jump into the freezing water. Your heart stops for a second. Your colleague is casually treading ice and discussing mortgage rates.
You climb out of the water, shivering violently. You realize a seagull just flew away with your only towel. The brutal January wind howls.
You book a 48-hour cruise on the 'Danskebåten' purely for cheap alcohol and the buffet. The Skagerrak sea is extremely rough and the ship is violently swaying.
You are at the buffet. The ship drops violently over a 10-meter wave. Your plate of pickled herring and roast beef flies directly into the Captain's lap.
You are banned from the buffet. To cope, you spend the rest of the trip in the duty-free shop buying 40 cartons of cigarettes you don't even smoke.
It is May. A 'Russebuss' (party bus for graduating 18-year-olds) with a 200,000-Watt sound system parks directly under your apartment window at 03:00 AM.
The police say 'let the kids celebrate'. The bass is vibrating your teeth. You go down in your bathrobe to confront 25 drunk teenagers.
They filmed you screaming in your bathrobe. You wake up as the #1 trending TikTok in Norway under the hashtag #SinteNabo (Angry Neighbor).
A colleague buys you a single black coffee from the canteen. It cost exactly 14 NOK. They stare at you expectantly.
You didn't pay them back. Three weeks later, you receive a Vipps request for 14 NOK, plus a passive-aggressive 2 NOK 'inflation adjustment'.
You declined the request. The colleague has now stopped saying 'Hei' in the hallway. The entire office has taken their side. You are isolated.
It is Sunday. It is practically illegal not to go for a 'Søndagstur' (Sunday hike). You pass a stranger on the muddy trail.
You said 'Hei!' with way too much enthusiasm. The stranger looks terrified, assuming you are a serial killer, and begins to speed-walk away.
To recover your dignity, you sit on a rock to eat your mandatory Kvikk Lunsj and orange. A flock of aggressive mountain seagulls targets your chocolate.
You have a mysterious, painful rash. You try to book an appointment with your 'Fastlege' (General Practitioner). The first available slot is in 6 weeks.
You waited 6 weeks. The rash is now covering your entire arm. You finally see the doctor, who looks at it for 4 seconds and prescribes 'Ibux and Paracet'.
You offended the doctor. They write a note in your permanent medical file stating you are 'dramatisk'. You are now permanently blacklisted from getting sick notes.
You are driving through the sub-sea Oslofjord tunnel. You accidentally drift 3 km/h over the speed limit. A red flash blinds you. The 'Fotoboks' got you.
Two weeks later, the physical letter arrives in your digital 'Altinn' mailbox. The fine is 3800 NOK for going 73 km/h in a 70 zone.
Your appeal claimed 'the wind pushed the car'. Statens Vegvesen replies with a mathematical proof showing that is impossible, and adds a 500 NOK administration fee.
The expensive Italian office coffee machine is out of beans. It is your turn to refill it, but you spill beans all over the floor right as the CEO walks in.
rightChoiceLabel: Pick them up one by one

text: The CEO steps on the loose beans, slips, and spills their scalding hot Earl Grey tea down their own shirt. The entire open-plan office gasps.
You are demoted to the basement archive room. Your only companion is a 1995 filter coffee machine that tastes like battery acid and despair.
You decide to vacuum your apartment. You check your watch. It is Sunday. In Norway, making noise on a 'Helligdag' (Holy day) is practically a war crime.
You vacuumed. Three minutes later, there is a furious knocking on your door. It is the elderly downstairs neighbor, Kjell, wielding a broom.
Kjell reports you to the housing board. You are summoned to a tribunal. You must publicly apologize and promise to only clean with silent microfiber cloths.
You are invited to a traditional pre-Christmas 'Lutefisk' dinner. You casually google it and learn it is dried cod soaked in caustic lye—literally drain cleaner.
The Lutefisk arrives. It jiggles on the plate like a translucent, fish-scented jelly. The host insists you eat it the traditional way: drowned in bacon fat and aquavit.
You swallowed the lye-fish. Your stomach immediately initiates a violent rebellion. You try to leave, but you are snowed in. The host offers you more fish.
It is July. The legendary 'Fellesferie' (Collective Vacation) has begun. The entire country has shut down. Your apartment's water pipe explodes.
You call 14 different plumbers. The voicemail for every single one says: 'We are at the cabin until August 15th. If the water is rising, learn to swim.'
You attempt to fix the plumbing yourself using a YouTube tutorial. You fail and flood the apartment below yours, which belongs to the head of the housing board.
You are snowed in with your Norwegian in-laws. They are watching 'Sakte-TV' (Slow TV): A live, 12-hour broadcast of firewood burning. No music, no talking.
It is Hour 7 of the fire broadcast. You are hallucinating from boredom. You casually suggest watching a movie instead. The room falls dead silent in extreme offense.
As punishment for your lack of attention span, your father-in-law switches the channel to the 134-hour broadcast of the Hurtigruten ship going up the coast.
You take a cheap flight to Svalbard in January to be edgy. It is pitch black 24/7, -30 degrees, and the locals require you to carry a rifle for polar bears.
You develop a severe, lingering cough. The local Svalbard doctor looks panicked and tells you it is literally illegal to die on the island because the permafrost pushes bodies back up.
The authorities are not taking any chances. You are deported back to the mainland on a cargo plane, strapped in next to a sedated walrus.
To fit in with your colleagues, you accept a pouch of 'Snus' (Nordic chewing tobacco) and put it under your upper lip. You immediately feel dizzy and nauseous.
You tried to keep it in, but someone asked you a question and you accidentally swallowed the entire Snus pouch during a budget meeting. Your face turns gray.
You vomit spectacularly all over the quarterly financial reports. To save face, you must pretend this was a profound, artistic statement on the weak Norwegian Krone.
It is March 2nd. The sun is out, but it is 2 degrees. Your colleagues insist on an 'Utepils' (outdoor beer) to celebrate spring.
You are shivering violently. A seagull targets your extremely expensive 120 NOK beer and knocks it all over your new wool sweater.
You caught mild hypothermia from the Utepils. You try to call in sick, but your boss quotes the proverb: 'There is no bad weather, only bad clothes.'
It is December. A national 'Smørkrise' (Butter Crisis) strikes again. The grocery store shelves are completely empty. Your Christmas baking is ruined.
You meet a shady guy in an alley behind REMA 1000. He sells you 2kg of 'premium butter'. At home, you realize it is just cheap Swedish margarine painted yellow.
You served the fake butter to your in-laws. Your mother-in-law notices immediately and calls your baking 'a crime against the nation'.
You sit next to someone on the train. Unfortunately, they are from Bergen. They immediately start talking to you loudly. In Norway, this is terrifying.
They ignore your clear boundaries and start passionately explaining why it rains better in Bergen than it does in Oslo. They expect a response.
The Bergenser declares you a lifelong friend and invites you to eat 'Fiskekaker' at Bryggen. You realize you can never ride this train route again.
You go for a casual Sunday hike in Nordmarka. You step off the path for 5 seconds to look at a weird mushroom and instantly lose all sense of direction.
Your phone dies because the battery froze at -5 degrees. You remember the 'Mountain Code' (Fjellvettreglene) says to dig a snow cave.
You dug a miserable snow cave. A Norwegian pensioner on cross-country skis stops by and asks if you are 'enjoying the fresh air'. They do not offer help.
You said 'Ja, deilig!' to save face. The pensioner skies away. You are too proud to call for help now. You live in the snow cave.
You are leaving a friend's cabin. You are handed the sacred 'Hyttebok' (Cabin Guestbook). In Norway, you are expected to write a poetic essay about how magical the trip was.
Your host reads your two-word 'Thanks' entry. They look at you as if you just burned the Norwegian flag. The silence in the cabin is deafening.
You are never invited back. As punishment, you are forced to spend your next Easter in the empty, silent, ghost-town city of Oslo while everyone else is in the mountains.
You board a Norwegian charter flight to 'Syden' (Gran Canaria) to escape the winter. The plane touches down safely. The entire cabin erupts into thunderous applause.
You unpack at the hotel. You realize you packed 3 logs of Brunost and 5 packs of Toro soup, but you forgot sunscreen.
You are sunburnt to a crisp on Day 1. You look like a boiled lobster. Other Norwegian tourists point and nod in solemn, shared understanding of your pain.
It is April. The annual 'Skattemelding' (Tax Return) is released. The Altinn website crashes immediately because 4 million people are checking it at the exact same second.
You finally log in. The screen glows red. You owe 45,000 NOK in 'Baksmell' (Back-tax). Your heart stops. Your vision blurs.
You realize you forgot to claim your 'Pendlerfradrag' (Commuter deduction). You adjust the form. Now the government owes YOU exactly 12 NOK.
You visit the famous Bergen Fish Market. A single, mediocre fish cake costs 250 NOK because it is a highly engineered tourist trap.
Before you can take a bite of your 250 NOK fish cake, a massive coastal seagull dive-bombs you, steals the food, and laughs in bird.
A German tourist filmed you screaming at the seagull. You go viral on TikTok as the 'Angry Fish Man'.
You invite your Norwegian colleagues over for 'Taco Friday'. Instead of using the sacred Old El Paso kit, you spend 12 hours slow-roasting pork and making authentic habanero salsa.
rightChoiceLabel: Panic and buy Old El Paso

text: You served the authentic tacos. Your colleagues stare at the table in absolute horror. One of them asks, with trembling lips, 'Where is the canned sweet corn? Where are the cucumber cubes?'
A colleague bravely bites into your authentic habanero salsa. Their Nordic palate, adapted only to boiled potatoes, immediately shatters. They are hyperventilating.
leftChoiceLabel: Offer them a glass of milk

text: It is -15°C outside. You dress in a thick Devold wool sweater and thermal 'Superundertøy' (long johns). You arrive at the office, but the thermostat is set to a tropical 25°C.
You kept the wool on. You are now in a 2-hour budget meeting. You are sweating profusely. Your face is purple. Taking off the sweater means exposing your stained undershirt.
You excuse yourself to the bathroom, desperately strip off your thermal long johns, and try to stuff them into your laptop bag. Your boss walks in while you are half-naked.
You accidentally parked 2 centimeters over the white line in the housing co-op. You find a laminated, highly detailed architectural schematic of the parking rules on your windshield.
You chose war. You leave a counter-note explaining that the line is faded and mathematically ambiguous. To establish dominance, you laminate your note twice.
The next morning, your car is entirely encased in 400 meters of industrial shrink wrap with a final note: 'Paragraph 3: Respect the Line. Sincerely, The Board.'
You are waiting for the elevator. You hear your very talkative neighbor approaching down the hall. In Norway, making small talk in an elevator is considered torture.
You spammed 'Close', but they stuck their hand in the door. You are now trapped in a 1.5 square meter metal box with them. They look at you and say 'Nice weather today.'
They break protocol. They ask a deeply personal question about your recent divorce. Norwegian law requires you to stare into the middle distance and simply mumble 'Uff da'.
You are visiting a 100-year-old cabin. The toilet is a 'Snurredass' (a giant composting carousel of human waste under the floor). It is your turn to manually spin the wheel.
You pull the lever, but the carousel is jammed. The cabin owner hands you a long, dark wooden stick and says 'You know what to do.'
You attempt to unjam the terrifying waste-mountain with the stick. You push too hard. The stick breaks in half. You drop the other half down the hole.
You bite into a frozen Grandiosa pizza crust and crack your molar in half. In Norway, healthcare is free, but adult dental care costs your mortal soul.
The Norwegian dentist looks at your tooth, sighs deeply, and hands you an estimate for 45,000 NOK. Do you pay it or book a cheap RyanAir flight to a clinic in Poland?
You took the cheap flight. You wake up in the Polish clinic with a perfect, shining ceramic crown. However, you notice a fresh surgical scar over your left kidney.
You are tired of paying 140 NOK for a single beer. You buy a highly illegal copper distillation kit from a guy named 'Ronny' on Finn.no to brew 'Hjemmebrent' (moonshine).
Your bathtub moonshine is finished. It smells like aviation fuel and pure despair. You take a sip to test it. Your vision immediately goes completely black for 45 seconds.
You served the aviation-moonshine at a neighborhood party. Three people are now communicating telepathically and the housing board president is outside trying to fistfight a moose.
You are at a job interview in Oslo. The panel asks what your greatest strength is. You accidentally say 'I am highly ambitious and a natural leader.' The panel gasps in absolute horror.
You have violated the Law of Jante. To recover the interview, you must convince them you are actually painfully average and enjoy eating unseasoned oatmeal in silence.
They offer you the job, but to ensure you do not think you are special, your title is 'Junior Assistant Synergist' and your desk faces a blank grey wall in the basement.
It is mid-January. The sun has not been seen since November. You buy a 10,000-lux anti-depression lamp and stare directly into it without blinking for 4 hours.
The extreme light exposure has triggered a manic episode. You walk into a Joker grocery store at 3 AM wearing only shorts and aggressively demand fresh strawberries.
The teenage cashier completely ignores your manic, half-naked breakdown. Without making eye contact, they scan your potatoes and simply ask: 'Vil du ha pose?' (Do you want a bag?)
It is December. Your mother-in-law casually asks if you have finished baking the 'Syv Slag' (7 traditional types of Christmas cookies). You only bought a plastic tub of Pepperkaker.
You lied. Now she is coming over for a taste test. You panic and try to deep-fry 'Fattigmann' dough in a wok. The boiling oil violently erupts, setting your kitchen curtains on fire.
The fire department puts out the blaze and fines you 5000 NOK. The Fire Chief steps over the ashes, looks at your burnt dough, and asks: 'Where are the other 6 cookies?'
You are cross-country skiing in Nordmarka, feeling athletic. Suddenly, a Norwegian dad aggressively overtakes you while dragging a 'Pulk' (sled) containing two toddlers and a Husky.
You chose to sprint. Your heart rate hits 195. You look over and the two toddlers in the sled are pointing and laughing at your terrible ski technique.
You collapse into the snow, coughing up a lung. The dad stops, looks down at you, hands you a lukewarm hotdog from a thermos, and tells you to 'stick to walking'.
You are invited to your nephew's 'Konfirmasjon' (coming-of-age ceremony). You put 500 NOK in an envelope. You then learn the standard gift is 15,000 NOK or stock options.
You handed over the 500 NOK. The 15-year-old opens the envelope, sighs aggressively, and places it in the 'disappointment pile' next to his new Rolex.
The teenager's parents corner you. You must now defend your financial poverty in front of the entire extended family while eating cold roast beef and potato salad.
You land at Gardermoen airport from abroad. You bought exactly 1 bottle of wine over the legal Tax-Free limit. You are sweating profusely as you approach the green 'Nothing to Declare' gate.
A massive Customs Dog (Tollhund) sits directly next to your bag. Your soul leaves your body. You mentally prepare yourself for 10 years in a high-security fjord prison.
The officer unzips your bag. He completely ignores the illegal wine, but confiscates the single non-declared raw potato you packed because of 'strict agricultural import terrorism laws'.
You are walking home in the pitch-black winter. A massive, 500kg Moose (Elg) is standing in your driveway eating fermented apples. It looks drunk and furious.
The drunk moose charges at you. You remember a tip from Reddit: 'Make yourself big and scream.' You open your jacket like a bat and yell Norwegian swear words.
The moose doesn't care. It knocks you into a snowbank, eats your mailbox, and wanders off. Your insurance company claims 'Acts of Drunk Forest Gods' are not covered.
You forgot your final Norwegian literature essay on Ibsen's 'A Doll's House'. You panic and use ChatGPT to write it 5 minutes before the deadline, submitting it without reading a single word.
Your teacher calls you in. They ask you to explain your essay's core thesis: 'Nora leaving Torvald represents the inevitable rise of Bitcoin and the blockchain.'
You are suspended for 'Aggressive Digital Plagiarism'. Your parents cancel your weekly allowance. You must now survive by shoveling heavy, wet snow for the entire neighborhood.
A highly-toxic, neon-colored energy drink promoted by YouTubers just dropped at Meny. A 12-year-old on a scooter is hoarding the last 5 cans in his basket.
You paid the 12-year-old. You chug the radioactive liquid right before gym class. Your resting heart rate hits 210 BPM and you can literally taste colors.
You collapse during the 3000-meter run. The gym teacher, a former Olympic cross-country skier with zero empathy, steps over your shaking body and yells 'Stop slacking!'
You decide to film a trending TikTok dance in the middle of Oslo Central Station during rush hour to achieve internet fame. Hundreds of tired commuters are staring.
While doing a spin, you accidentally smack a grumpy commuter holding a 75 NOK Narvesen coffee. The scalding liquid goes everywhere.
The commuter films you. You become a national meme titled 'The Cringe of Oslo S'. Teenagers point and laugh at you on the subway.
You are hiding in the school bathroom hitting a mango-flavored vape. The fire alarm suddenly triggers. 800 people are now evacuating into the -15°C blizzard outside.
The principal is standing outside with the fire department reviewing security footage to see who left the bathroom last. You are prime suspect number one.
You successfully framed a 13-year-old. The guilt eats your soul. The school counselor, thinking you are a good influence, forces you to mentor the kid you just ruined.
You try to flip a 'vintage' 90s Helly Hansen jacket on Finn.no for 4000 NOK. A buyer named 'Sondre' insists on meeting at a sketchy gas station at 23:30.
'Sondre' turns out to be a 45-year-old man who just wants to talk. He traps you in a 2-hour conversation about how the 90s trance music scene was superior. He hasn't looked at the jacket.
You walk away empty-handed in the freezing rain. You reach into your pocket and realize Sondre pickpocketed your AirPods while explaining the cultural impact of Darude Sandstorm.
You are a respected politician at the Gardermoen Airport Tax-Free. You see a pair of Hugo Boss sunglasses. The urge to commit petty theft overwhelms your rational brain.
You stole them. A polite security guard stops you. You claim you were 'distracted by state affairs.' The security footage shows you meticulously chewing the security tag off with your teeth.
You are forced to resign in national disgrace. On the bright side, the Norwegian parliamentary rules grant you 12 months of full pay to sit at home and reflect on your eyewear.
You are invited to a highly controversial Royal Wedding in Geiranger. The groom is a Hollywood Shaman. To enter the tent, you must buy his 'Spirit-Optimizing Medallion'.
You bought the medallion. It is made of cheap plastic and immediately gives you a severe neck rash. The Shaman approaches and says the rash is 'dark toxins leaving your aura'.
You demanded a refund. The Shaman's PR team immediately threatens to sue you for disrupting his cosmic vibrations. You must now wear the rash-medallion forever to avoid bankruptcy.
The Norwegian Krone (NOK) has hit a historic low. You take the ferry to Copenhagen for a 'cheap' weekend. You order two beers and a hotdog. The bill is 350 Danish Kroner.
You tapped your card. Your Norwegian bank app sends an immediate fraud alert: 'WARNING: 350 DKK is now roughly equivalent to a small mortgage. Are you sure?'
You return to Norway financially ruined from buying three items in Denmark. You must now sustain yourself entirely on foraging wild blueberries and drinking tap water until next payday.
You are appointed as the Minister of Higher Education. A 19-year-old student discovers your Master's thesis is entirely copy-pasted from Wikipedia and a 2014 Reddit post.
You hold a press conference. The journalists ask why your thesis includes the phrase 'Edit: Thanks for the gold, kind stranger!' You panic.
You resign in disgrace. However, this is Norway. The government immediately gives you a newly created, higher-paying job as the 'Senior Consultant of Academic Integrity'.
Your small tech startup makes a tiny profit. Terrified of the new Norwegian 'Wealth Tax', you copy the billionaires and immediately relocate your family to a tiny village in Switzerland.
You live in a massive Swiss chalet, saving 2% on taxes. But you are deeply depressed because you can't find Grandiosa or Kvikk Lunsj anywhere. The Swiss chocolate tastes 'too rich'.
You pay a smuggler 50,000 NOK to bring you a single block of Brunost across the Alps. The Swiss police intercept the package and classify it as an illegal biological weapon.
US President Donald Trump officially announces on live TV that he is buying Svalbard to build an Arctic golf course. The Norwegian Prime Minister is panicking and asks for your advice.
You advised the Prime Minister to sell. The transaction clears. You are given a massive finder's fee in USD, but you are now the most hated person in Norwegian history.
Trump cancels the check. He just found out Svalbard only has a frozen seed vault and a ban on dying. You are left with the massive Norwegian tax bill on your 'hypothetical' income.
The Norwegian Krone has crashed so drastically that the Danish Krone is worth double. A 14-year-old Danish kid uses his paper route savings to buy your entire apartment building in Oslo.
The Danish teenager demands you pay your rent in 'Kamelåså' and Lego sets. When you ask what a 'Kamelåså' is, he threatens to evict you.
You shoved a literal hot potato in your mouth to mimic the Danish accent and appease your landlord. You immediately choke and pass out in the hallway.
Global tensions are peaking. You are unexpectedly drafted into 'Heimevernet' (The Home Guard). You are ordered to deploy to the Russian border on the same night as the Eurovision finale.
You are in a frozen trench in Finnmark holding a rifle from 1982. Foreign GPS jammers are messing with your phone, and your Tinder location says you are in Murmansk.
You matched with an enemy operative. They offer to trade highly classified state military secrets for a single package of genuine Norwegian 'Kviteseid Smør' (premium butter).
The government forces your tiny municipality to merge with the neighboring town you despise. The new Mayor immediately bans all local dialects and mandates 'Standard Eastern Norwegian'.
You start a rebellion armed with pitchforks and stale Kneippbrød. The riot police arrive in their brand-new electric squad cars, but the batteries instantly die in the -20 degree cold.
You agree to let the police plug their squad cars into your house. You charge them exactly 500 NOK per kWh because you are a brutal capitalist.
The EU passes a green directive requiring Norway to export 95% of its hydropower to the continent. Your monthly electricity bill hits 45,000 NOK. It is -15 degrees inside your living room.
You illegally tapped into the municipal streetlamp to run a tiny 400-watt heater. The newly formed 'Strømpoliti' (Electricity Police) raid your apartment wearing tactical thermal goggles.
You are found guilty of 'Energy Treason'. You are sentenced to 400 hours of forced labor running on a giant hamster wheel in the Parliament basement to power the politicians' espresso machines.
Donald Trump imposes a 500% tariff on Norwegian salmon because the Prime Minister refused to sell him a famous fjord for a golf resort. The Norwegian economy instantly crashes.
To lift the tariff, the government caves in and forces your construction firm to build a massive, 50-story golden 'Trump Tower' directly on top of the fragile Briksdal glacier.
Global warming accelerates. The glacier melts rapidly, sending the half-built golden tower sliding directly into the deep fjord. The US President blames you personally on social media.
VG newspaper exposes your local Mayor for secretly spending 3 million NOK in taxpayer money to hire a 'spiritual aura cleanser' for the municipal reindeer herd.
You are the Mayor's PR advisor. They refuse to step down, claiming the reindeer's chakra alignment is absolutely vital for regional stability. The national press is waiting outside.
You told the Mayor to double down. They attempt to perform a live, televised aura-cleansing on a massive bull reindeer. The reindeer immediately kicks the Mayor directly in the jaw.
You are ice fishing in Finnmark. A highly suspicious, muscular Beluga whale wearing a tactical harness surfaces and offers you 50,000 Rubles for your Wi-Fi password.
You gave the whale your Wi-Fi. The next morning, PST (Norwegian Intelligence Service) kicks down your door. You are formally charged with 'Aiding an Aquatic Foreign Operative'.
To avoid a 15-year treason sentence, PST recruits you as a double agent. Your mission is to infiltrate a foreign naval base by swimming across the freezing border in a highly realistic Cod costume.

You are invited to an exclusive 'afterparty' at the Royal Estate by the Crown Princess's adult son. You arrive to find him frantically shredding documents and vacuuming white powder off a priceless antique rug.
You helped vacuum. Suddenly, a high-ranking Royal Guard knocks on the door and tells you the police will arrive in exactly 14 minutes for a 'completely unannounced and spontaneous' raid.
You are hiding in the royal closet. You accidentally step on a crate of the Shaman's 'Spirit-Optimizing Medallions'. The loud plastic crunch alerts the police.
It is August. You are at 'Arendalsuka', the massive political festival where PR consultants pretend to care about the working class while drinking 200 NOK organic cider on yachts.
You rented the yacht. The Minister of Finance steps aboard. He tells you the economy is doing great, then immediately asks to borrow 500 NOK for a late-night kebab.
The Minister eats your kebab and promises you a government grant for your new startup: an app that tracks how many times politicians say 'Vi må ta debatten' (We must take the debate).
A major government minister is caught copying 95% of their Master's thesis. You are hired as their crisis PR manager. The media is demanding an explanation for the identical typos.
You claimed it was a 'Text Anomaly'. A journalist discovers the minister even copied a recipe for Toro Brown Sauce into chapter 4. The nation is in an uproar.
rightChoiceLabel: Say the sauce is a metaphor

text: The minister is forced to resign in disgrace. But as is Norwegian tradition, they are immediately appointed to a highly paid board position at the state railway company. They offer you a job.
Norwegians love useless World Records. You decide to break the record for 'Longest Distance Skied on Asphalt in July'. You put on roller skis and hit the E18 highway.
The asphalt is literally melting in the 28-degree heat. Your roller skis sink into the tar. An angry truck driver hauling 10 tons of frozen salmon is honking at you.
You survived the ditch, but the police give you a 12,000 NOK fine for disrupting traffic. The local newspaper runs a front-page story: 'Local Hero Robbed of Glory by The State.'
Jens Stoltenberg has dramatically returned from NATO to 'save' Norwegian politics. The entire nation enters a trance-like state of nostalgia. You are asked to swear allegiance to the 'Stoltenback'.
You said he is overrated. Your coworkers form a circle around your desk, chanting his old speeches and aggressively drinking Friele coffee until you publicly apologize.
You refused. You are officially exiled to Sweden. Your Norwegian passport is revoked, and you are forced to assemble IKEA furniture for minimum wage while eating fermented herring.

The government introduces AI-powered 'Bompenger' (toll booths) that charge you based on your carbon footprint. You sigh heavily in traffic. The AI registers your sigh as a 'carbon-heavy exhalation' and charges you 500 NOK.
You held your breath to avoid emissions, passed out, and crashed into the toll booth. The AI charges you 2 million NOK for 'unauthorized demolition of state infrastructure'.
Sweden denies your asylum because you do not have enough 'fika' experience. You are deported back to the AI toll booth, which is now demanding a public apology on national TV.
A famous Norwegian salmon billionaire who fled to Switzerland sneaks back into Oslo in disguise because he misses 'Fjordland' microwave meals. You spot him at the local Kiwi.
You demand 1 million NOK to keep quiet. He counters by offering you 500 kg of frozen salmon and a rusty Rolex he claims was owned by the King.
You took the salmon and filled your tiny Oslo apartment with it. The freezer breaks. The horrific smell alerts the housing board, who deploy a biological hazmat team.
NATO hosts a massive winter drill in Troms. US Marines arrive without winter gear. The Norwegian commander orders you to teach them how to survive using only a Kvikk Lunsj and a stick.
An American general misunderstands your instruction, eats the stick, and tries to fire the Kvikk Lunsj from his rifle. He aggressively blames you for sabotaging allied forces.
The manual is written in heavily dialected Nynorsk. The Pentagon assumes it is a secret Russian cipher. You are temporarily detained by the CIA in a snowbank.
To stop Norwegians from buying cheap bacon, Sweden builds a massive wall and tries to make Norway pay for it in oil. You attempt to dig a tunnel under it to get cheap Pepsi Max.
Your tunnel collapses. You emerge directly inside a Swedish Systembolaget (liquor store). The Swedish manager stares at you, covered in dirt and holding a shovel.
The manager forces you to assemble a 400-piece IKEA shelving unit before he lets you leave without calling the border guards. You realize you lost the Allen wrench.
The Norwegian Parliament replaces a scandal-ridden minister with an AI language model to save money. The AI immediately raises taxes on 'happiness' to balance the budget.
You acted depressed to avoid the tax. The AI registers your depression and automatically enrolls you in a mandatory 6-month 'Forest Therapy' camp in the deep woods of Hedmark.
At the forest camp, you are forced to hug birch trees 12 hours a day. The mosquitoes are the size of small birds. You realize this is the government's true plan to solve the pension crisis.
You get a job managing a small portfolio for the Norwegian Sovereign Wealth Fund. You accidentally invest 500 billion NOK into a meme coin based on a picture of a Swedish dog.
The meme coin crashes 99%. You must explain to Parliament why the national pension fund for the next three generations is now completely worthless.
You suggested selling the entire city of Bergen to a tech billionaire to recoup the losses. Parliament unanimously agrees. Your social standing skyrockets in Oslo, though Bergen is furious.
You are driving an electric car across the Swedish border with 50kg of cheap bacon hidden in the battery compartment. A customs drone spots your suspension sagging heavily.
The customs officers stop you. They deploy elite dogs trained specifically to sniff out non-taxed Swedish pork products. The dogs surround your car and bark aggressively.
You are found guilty of 'Bacon Treason'. You must spend the next five years eating exclusively Norwegian boiled cod without any seasoning to cleanse your palate.
You are furious at the new toll road prices. You protest by supergluing your hands to a major 'Bompenger' toll booth in Oslo during the morning rush hour.
The temperature drops to -15 degrees. The superglue freezes solid. You are now permanently attached to the toll booth as angry commuters drive by, throwing frozen coins at your head.
You actually made 5000 NOK in thrown coins. The Tax Authority (Skatteetaten) immediately arrives and reclassifies your frozen body as a commercial toll operator.
You are a reindeer herder in Finnmark. One of your reindeer crosses the Russian border. The Russian state demands a 50 million Ruble fine for 'illegal grazing and espionage'.
You ignored the fine. A diplomatic crisis erupts. The Norwegian Prime Minister begs you to sneak across the border at night to retrieve the reindeer to avoid a global conflict.
You sneak into the Russian military camp. You find your reindeer, but it is now wearing a tiny Russian general's uniform and refuses to return to the Norwegian side.
The Norwegian government accidentally transfers the entire national arts budget to your personal bank account. It is 800 million NOK.
You fled to Dubai with the arts budget. The Norwegian state sends an elite team of cultural critics to hunt you down and lecture you on modern theatrical dance.
The cultural critics corner you. They force you to watch a 6-hour avant-garde performance piece about the anxiety of eating brown cheese. You surrender the money to make it stop.
You inherit a cabin in Finnmark, sitting exactly on the Russian border. Vladimir Putin crosses the border shirtless on a horse and declares your wooden sauna sovereign Russian territory.
You offered him a towel. He refuses, citing Western weakness, and challenges you to a 10-hour ice-bathing contest in the freezing Pasvik river to determine the true owner.
The local sheriff (Lensmann) arrives, but says he cannot intervene because Putin just paid his Norwegian municipal property tax on the sauna via the Vipps app. You are legally evicted.
Kim Jong Un visits Oslo in secret to study the mind-control properties of the 'Pizza Grandiosa'. The government assigns you as his official pizza guide to avoid an international incident.
You served it perfectly. He takes a massive bite immediately out of the oven and suffers third-degree burns on the roof of his mouth. Furious, he reaches for the nuclear launch button.
The extreme sugar rush of the Norwegian Solo soda calms him down. However, he now demands to buy the entire Stabburet pizza factory using illicit crypto.
President Erdogan blocks Norway's latest NATO initiative until the Norwegian government formally apologizes for the existence of 'Fårikål' (the national sheep and cabbage stew).
You defended the stew on national TV. Erdogan responds by banning all Norwegians from traveling to Alanya for their cheap summer holidays. Millions of sunburned Norwegians riot in Oslo.
To stop the riots, the Prime Minister forces you to travel to Ankara and personally eat three massive bowls of garlic kebab sauce while singing the Turkish national anthem to apologize.
Donald Trump claims the Norwegian wind turbines in Trøndelag are killing non-existent bald eagles. He sends a team of aggressive American lawyers to legally dismantle the wind farm.
You sabotaged the lawyers by feeding them 2-year-old expired 'Rakfisk' (fermented trout). They are all hospitalized with severe hallucinations, claiming they saw socialist ghosts.
Trump is furious and places a total embargo on all Norwegian exports. The country descends into complete anarchy as the strategic national reserves of Pepsi Max run dry in just 48 hours.
The UN initiates a 'Dictator Exchange Program'. Your small municipality is forced to host a notorious authoritarian leader for a week. He demands a massive military parade down Karl Johan street.
You force the dictator to participate in the spring 'Dugnad' (community labor). He is handed a rusty rake and ordered to clear wet leaves by a terrifying 70-year-old housing board president.
The dictator's spirit is completely broken by the relentless passive-aggression of Norwegian community management. He resigns his presidency and applies for a job at the local Kiwi supermarket.
Elon Musk buys a pristine mountain in Lofoten to build a massive server farm. He hires you and demands you work a 100-hour 'hardcore' week. In Norway, this is a crime against humanity.
You called the Labor Authority (Arbeidstilsynet). They arrive in a slow electric boat, armed with clipboards and a legally mandated 30-minute waffle break schedule.
Musk tries to fire the inspectors via a late-night tweet. The Norwegian government responds by putting him in a 'timeout room' until he successfully builds an IKEA desk without a manual.
King Charles is visiting Oslo. While waving from a yacht in the Oslofjord, he drops a priceless royal crown into the freezing water. He points directly at you to fetch it.
You dive in and retrieve the crown, but a territorial Norwegian salmon has already laid eggs inside it. The King is disgusted and threatens to sanction all Norwegian fish exports.
To prevent a British trade war, you offer the King a massive block of 'Brunost'. He bites into it thinking it is sweet fudge, immediately gags, and declares war on Norway.
A famous Swedish climate activist blocks the entrance to the Vinmonopolet (liquor store). It is Friday at 17:30. If you don't get your wine now, your weekend is ruined.
You stop to listen. She spots your plastic bag full of non-recycled batteries and singles you out. You are globally shamed on a livestream watched by 4 million people.
rightChoiceLabel: Eat a battery to assert dominance

text: To recover your public image, you are forced to join her protest. You miss your sacred Friday Taco night and must now live in a wind-powered tent outside Stortinget.
US President Joe Biden visits Oslo for a NATO summit. He wanders away from his security detail and gets hopelessly lost in the Nationaltheatret subway station.
You find him eating a stale, 3-day-old bacon hotdog from Narvesen. The Secret Service suddenly surrounds you, demanding to know if the hotdog is poisoned with 'socialized medicine'.
The CIA detains you in a black SUV. To buy your freedom, you must bribe the agents with your emergency stash of Kvikk Lunsj. They put you on a permanent American snack-smuggler watch list.
You visit Kristiansand, the heart of the Norwegian 'Bible Belt'. You accidentally swear loudly when you drop your soft-serve ice cream. The entire town gasps and stops moving.
The local conservative mayor arrests you for 'moral decay'. You are sentenced to 40 hours of mandatory church choir practice to cleanse your corrupted urban soul.
You get bored during choir practice and try to spice up the solemn hymn with a heavy metal vocal growl. You are violently chased out of the county by angry grandmothers wielding wooden spoons.

Chinese President Xi Jinping gifts Norway two giant pandas as a diplomatic token. They arrive at Gardermoen, but Mattilsynet (Food Safety Authority) intercepts them for lacking the correct EU rabies documentation.
You followed the rules. Mattilsynet threatens to euthanize the pandas to protect the local ecosystem. The Chinese embassy immediately prepares for total economic warfare.
You hide the pandas in your tiny Oslo apartment while forging their paperwork. They consume your expensive bamboo flooring and destroy your deposit. You are now broke, but have averted WW3.
French President Emmanuel Macron flees the violent Paris pension riots and seeks asylum in Norway. He shows up at a local NAV office demanding a luxury retirement package at age 62.
You hand Macron 'Form 11-C' and inform him the Norwegian retirement age is 67. Furious, he attempts to incite a violent riot in the NAV lobby.
The riot fails. The Norwegian retirees in the lobby completely ignore him, drinking stale filter coffee and waiting for their queue number. Macron's spirit breaks and he sits down quietly.
Canadian PM Justin Trudeau visits Oslo. To painfully over-pander to the locals, he shows up to a formal diplomatic dinner wearing full Black Metal corpse paint and a spiked collar.
He gets inside. Trying to prove his 'Norwegian authenticity', Trudeau lights a ceremonial candle. He accidentally sets the 300-year-old wooden banquet hall on fire.
You saved the buffet but the hall burned down. The state blames you for letting Trudeau in. You must pay 40 million NOK or serve 5 years in Halden Prison.
Pope Francis visits Nidaros Cathedral. During a massive public mass, his assistant makes a horrible mistake and replaces the communion wafers with thin slices of Gudbrandsdalsost (Brown Cheese).
The Pope eats the cheese. It immediately sticks to the roof of his mouth. He cannot speak for 15 minutes, sweating profusely. The crowd assumes he is having a profound divine revelation.
The Vatican officially declares Brunost a holy relic. The price skyrockets to 5000 NOK per kilo overnight. You are financially ruined, but spiritually fulfilled.
Donald Trump discovers the Norwegian Sovereign Wealth Fund has 1.5 trillion dollars. He calls the Prime Minister at 3 AM to pitch a 'tremendous, very beautiful' casino in Svalbard.
The Prime Minister panicked and transferred the call to you. Trump promises to make the polar bears 'pay for the wall'. You must politely decline using Norwegian corporate jargon.
Trump takes immense personal offense to your corporate jargon. He sends Navy SEALs to confiscate your personal checking account. You lose your entire life savings of 412 NOK.
Greta Thunberg boards your North Sea oil rig in a rubber dinghy. She superglues herself to the main drill and demands you shut down all Norwegian oil production immediately.
You offer her waffles to defuse the tension. She takes a bite and asks if they are vegan. You used 'Tine Meierismør' (real butter). She begins screaming through a megaphone.
You lied about the butter. A coworker exposes you on TikTok. You are fired by Equinor, canceled globally, and fined by the Food Safety Authority for mislabeling baked goods.
Former UK PM Boris Johnson washes ashore on your private beach in southern Norway in a dinghy. He begs for a secret, post-Brexit trade deal involving only raw cod and butter.
You sell him 500kg of cod. He pays you entirely in devalued British Pounds and gives you a signed, waterlogged copy of his biography.
You take the Pounds to a Norwegian bank. The teller laughs uncontrollably, calls her manager over to laugh, and offers you a single half-eaten Kvikk Lunsj for the cash.
A sanctioned Russian Oligarch's 500-foot mega-yacht is seized in the Oslofjord. The state assigns you to guard it. The oligarch offers you 20 million NOK to 'take a nap'.
You took the bribe and napped. The yacht attempts to flee but immediately crashes into a tiny, invisible Norwegian underwater rock (skjær). It is sinking rapidly.
You rescue him, but the authorities catch you both. You are thrown into Halden Prison. The oligarch complains daily to you that the flat-screen TV in his cell is only 40 inches.
EU President Ursula von der Leyen decrees that Norwegian 'Brunost' must be legally renamed to 'Brown Sweet Milk Block' because it is technically not cheese.
You burned the flag. The EU retaliates by embargoing all Norwegian salmon. The fjords are now overflowing with millions of unsold, deeply depressed fish.
To solve the crisis, you mandate your family must eat 5kg of salmon daily. After three weeks, your skin turns bright orange and you constantly crave the ocean.
Mark Zuckerberg visits Finnmark. He offers you a lifetime Metaverse premium subscription if you sell him your sacred reindeer grazing land for a massive server farm.
You refuse. Zuckerberg threatens to permanently delete your Facebook account. You panic, realizing the local housing cooperative group is your only source of human drama.
You caved and sold. The massive server farm is built. However, your angry reindeer chew through the main fiber optic cable, taking down global Facebook for 48 hours. You are a national hero.
